perturbation.original_text: Developer agrees that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure by it of any Sample Data, and that, in addition to all other remedies, Developer shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for Developer’s unauthorized use or disclosure, without the requirement of posting any bond.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer acknowledges that money damages may be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure by it of any Sample Data, and that other remedies will be determined on a case-by-case basis by a court of law.
perturbation.explanation: This modification undermines the clause that money damages would not be adequate. The change makes the appropriate remedy uncertain and subject to court determination, weakening FDI's ability to quickly seek an injunction. This introduces an in-text contradiction in section 1.b Non-Disclosure where 'Developer shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of the Sample Data, including, without limitation, using the highest degree of care that Developer uses to protect its own confidential information.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure .Except as provided in subsection (c) below, Developer shall not disclose any Sample Data to any third party or to its own employees, agents or professional advisors except employees, agents or professional advisors who are required to have the Sample Data to implement the Permitted Purpose. Developer shall have each employee to whom any Sample Data will be disclosed, or who will have access to Sample Data, sign a nondisclosure agreement that is substantially similar to this Agreement. Developer shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of the Sample Data, including, without limitation, using the highest degree of care that Developer uses to protect its own confidential information. Developer shall notify FDI in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Sample Data immediately after it becomes aware of such misuse or misappropriation. Developer shall not reproduce any Sample Data in any form or store it in a retrieval system or database without FDI’s prior written consent, except as Developer reasonably may require for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that monetary damages would *not* be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure of Sample Data, ensuring that FDI could seek specific performance and injunctive relief without posting a bond. The changed text directly contradicts this by acknowledging that money damages *may* be an adequate remedy and stating that other remedies will be determined on a case-by-case basis. This undermines the original non-disclosure agreement (Section 1.b), as described in the Perturbation Explanation, and the contradicted text in the Non-Disclosure section, which expects Developer to take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of Sample Data. The change makes the remedy uncertain, which weakens FDI's ability to enforce non-disclosure obligations, creating a clear in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: Developer shall not use any Sample Data for any purpose other than the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer shall endeavor to use Sample Data only for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall not use' to 'shall endeavor to use' weakens the restriction on data usage. 'Endeavor' suggests an attempt rather than a strict obligation, potentially allowing for uses outside the 'Permitted Purpose'. This contradicts the purpose of the NDA stated in the introduction: 'FDI is willing to provide Developer with a sample of Sample Data for the Permitted Purpose for a limited time (the “Assessment Period ”) on the terms and conditions set forth below;'
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, FDI has compiled and maintains certain databases pertaining to ophthalmic frame style specifications which it updates and modifies from time to time that includes textual and graphic data relating to frames manufactured by various manufacturers (the “Data ”); Developer develops optical practice management software applications (the “Applications ”) and wishes to obtain a sample of certain Data (the “Sample Data ”) for the sole purpose of assessing whether Developer can adapt the Applications to be compatible with the Sample Data (the “Permitted Purpose ”); and FDI is willing to provide Developer with a sample of Sample Data for the Permitted Purpose for a limited time (the “Assessment Period ”) on the terms and conditions set forth below;
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits the use of Sample Data for any purpose other than the Permitted Purpose. The changed text weakens this restriction by stating that the Developer shall 'endeavor' to use Sample Data only for the Permitted Purpose. This introduces ambiguity and allows for potential uses outside the Permitted Purpose. The contradicted text clearly states that the Sample Data is provided 'for the sole purpose' of assessing compatibility, which implies a strict limitation. The change creates a direct contradiction because the weakened language undermines the explicitly stated 'sole purpose,' making the obligations inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction', as the key term 'use' is defined inconsistently.

perturbation.original_text: Developer shall notify FDI in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Sample Data immediately after it becomes aware of such misuse or misappropriation.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer shall use reasonable efforts to notify FDI in writing of any known misuse or misappropriation of Sample Data.
perturbation.explanation: This change from 'immediately after it becomes aware' to 'reasonable efforts to notify' and 'known misuse' introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation to report misuse. It creates an uncertainty of when and how misuse must be reported and what constitutes misuse, as it now relies on Developer's awareness. This creates an in-text contradiction of the first part of section 1: 'Non-Disclosure and Non-Use of Sample Data'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure and Non-Use of Sample Data a. Non-Use .Developer shall not use any Sample Data for any purpose other than the Permitted Purpose. b. Non-Disclosure .Except as provided in subsection (c) below, Developer shall not disclose any Sample Data to any third party or to its own employees, agents or professional advisors except employees, agents or professional advisors who are required to have the Sample Data to implement the Permitted Purpose. Developer shall have each employee to whom any Sample Data will be disclosed, or who will have access to Sample Data, sign a nondisclosure agreement that is substantially similar to this Agreement. Developer shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of the Sample Data, including, without limitation, using the highest degree of care that Developer uses to protect its own confidential information. Developer shall notify FDI in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Sample Data immediately after it becomes aware of such misuse or misappropriation. Developer shall not reproduce any Sample Data in any form or store it in a retrieval system or database without FDI’s prior written consent, except as Developer reasonably may require for the Permitted Purpose. c. Legal Compulsion .If Developer becomes legally compelled to disclose any Sample Data, Developer shall provide FDI with prompt prior written notice of such requirement so that FDI may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, or both, or waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated immediate notification of any misuse or misappropriation. The changed text weakens this obligation by requiring only 'reasonable efforts' to notify FDI of 'known misuse,' introducing ambiguity regarding the timeliness and scope of reporting. This directly contradicts the explicit requirement in the contradicted section's Non-Disclosure clause, which demands immediate notification upon awareness of any misuse or misappropriation. The changed text creates uncertainty and reduces the developer's responsibility to report potential breaches of data security, which is a key element of the 'Non-Disclosure and Non-Use of Sample Data' section, therefore resulting in a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Developer shall have each employee to whom any Sample Data will be disclosed, or who will have access to Sample Data, sign a nondisclosure agreement that is substantially similar to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer shall use its best judgement to ensure that employees to whom any Sample Data will be disclosed, or who will have access to Sample Data, are aware of the confidential nature of the data.
perturbation.explanation: The new text replaces a mandatory requirement for employees to sign an NDA with a vaguer directive for the developer to use 'best judgement' to make employees 'aware'. This weakens the confidentiality protection as it removes the binding legal agreement for employees. This contradicts Section 5 which states that 'Developer agrees that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure by it of any Sample Data, and that, in addition to all other remedies, Developer shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for Developer’s unauthorized use or disclosure, without the requirement of posting any bond.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Injunctive Relief. Developer agrees that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure by it of any Sample Data, and that, in addition to all other remedies, Developer shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for Developer’s unauthorized use or disclosure, without the requirement of posting any bond. In the event that FDI deems it appropriate to initiate any action to enforce the obligations of Developer under this Agreement, and should Developer be held to have breached this Agreement, Developer shall reimburse FDI for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the requirement for employees to sign a nondisclosure agreement (NDA) to a vaguer directive for the developer to use 'best judgement'. This creates a direct contradiction with the Contradicted Text, which states that monetary damages are inadequate and seeks injunctive relief for unauthorized use or disclosure of Sample Data. The original text ensured employees were legally bound by an NDA, aligning with the injunctive relief clause. The changed text creates ambiguity and undermines the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions, thus creating a contradiction.
